cee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter C / c.

core: noun. In general usage, an essential part of a thing surrounded by other essential things.

decor: noun. The style of decoration of a room or building.

doe: noun. A female deer; also used of similar animals such as antelope (less commonly a goat, as nanny is also used).
doer: noun. Someone who does, performs, or executes; an active person, an agent.
dor: noun. Any of species Geotrupes stercorarius of large European dung beetles that make a droning noise while flying.
dree: verb. To bear or endure (something); to put up with, to suffer, to undergo.
erode: verb. To wear away by abrasion, corrosion, or chemical reaction.
erred: verb. simple past and past participle of err
error: noun. The state, quality, or condition of being wrong.

orc: noun. Any of several large, ferocious sea creatures, now especially the killer whale.
orderer: noun. A person who orders, or who places an order
ore: noun. Rock or other material that contains valuable or utilitarian materials; primarily a rock containing metals or gems for which it is
recode: verb. To code again or differently.
record: noun. An item of information put into a temporary or permanent physical medium.
recorder: noun. An apparatus for recording; a device which records.

ree: noun. Alternative form of rei (“Portuguese real”).
reed: noun. Any of various types of tall stiff perennial grass-like plants growing together in groups near water.
reorder: verb. To place in a new order; to rearrange.
roc: noun. An enormous mythical bird in Eastern legend.
rod: noun. A straight, round stick, shaft, bar, cane, or staff.
cdr: noun. The second part of a cons in Lisp.
ceo: noun. Alternative letter-case form of CEO.
cro: noun. Synonym of bro (“a male comrade or friend”).
